name: human_reanchor
description: Scenario (g): the agent RE-ANCHORS a stored human annotation (same id, different snippet) and attaches a moderation_flag to its copy. Fix F7: _preserve_human matches by id FIRST and REPLACES the altered copy in place — the final output contains exactly ONE copy (the stored original), never an ai-moderated duplicate alongside a re-appended human. Fix F14: the agent's moderation_flag is harvested into ladder.moderation_flags before the restore strips it.
title: Eval Eta Numbers

sections.heading: (lead)
paragraphs: In mathematics, an eta number is a number with the eta property. Equivalently, an eta value arises from the eta map.

anchor.snippet: an eta number is a number

annotations.label: Eta number (human)

anchor.snippet: an eta value arises from the eta map

annotations.moderation_flag: anchor may be wrong — the definition moved to the second sentence

anchor.snippet: an eta number is a number

notes: F7: before the fix, the re-anchored copy missed the sig match, so the stored human was APPENDED alongside the agent's altered copy — a duplicate that would then 422 (altered human on the wire) or double-render. The id-first match replaces in place (ladder restored=1).

F14: the moderation_flag the agent attached to its copy is harvested as [id, flag] into ladder.moderation_flags before the restore strips it; moderate.py prints it in the run log and it rides revisions.meta.

moderation_flags: anchor may be wrong — the definition moved to the second sentence
